name: Python Error Handling Assembly
description: Assemble common try/except/finally and validation patterns.

questions.question: Assemble a basic try-except catching ValueError.
Assemble exception binding to variable e.
questions.explanation: Use as to bind the exception object.

questions.question: Assemble finally block header.
questions.explanation: finally runs whether an exception occurred or not.

questions.question: Assemble raise statement for ValueError with message.
questions.explanation: raise is Python's keyword for exceptions.

questions.question: Assemble assertion checking x is non-negative.
questions.explanation: assert is useful for internal sanity checks.

questions.question: Assemble guarded int conversion from text s.
questions.explanation: Wrap parsing in try/except to handle bad input.

questions.question: Assemble code to re-raise current exception.
questions.explanation: Bare raise inside except rethrows the current exception.

questions.question: Assemble code to open file safely with context manager.
questions.explanation: with ensures the file is closed automatically.

questions.question: Assemble condition that raises if user is not found.
Assemble except tuple catching both KeyError and IndexError.
questions.explanation: Multiple exception types can be grouped in a tuple.

questions.question: Assemble else block header used after successful try.
questions.explanation: else executes only when try block raises no exception.

questions.question: Assemble expression to get dict key safely with default 0.
questions.explanation: get avoids KeyError by supplying a fallback.

questions.question: Assemble condition checking value is not None before use.
questions.explanation: Use is not None for explicit None checks.

questions.question: Assemble code to catch broad Exception as e.
questions.explanation: Catching Exception is broad and should be used carefully.

questions.question: Assemble cleanup call in finally block.
questions.explanation: Close resources in finally to ensure cleanup.
